name: Celebration Rugelach- Country Living
recipeIngredient: Fresh ivy or other green leaves (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Pastry in 2 batches: In a large bowl, combine half the flour and baking powder; mix well. With an electric mixer on low speed, beat in half the butter and cream cheese until smooth dough forms. Divide the dough into 4 balls. Repeat with remaining flour, baking powder, butter, and cream cheese to make 4 more balls of dough. Wrap the balls of dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 2 hours or overnight.
2. On a lightly floured surface, roll one ball of dough into a 12 by 10 inch rectangle, about 1/8 inch thick. With a fluted pastry wheel or knife, cut the rectangle into two 12 by 5-inch strips. Using a pastry wheel, trim 1/16 inch from outside lengthwise edges to flute. Transfer the strips to a baking sheet or large plate, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate. Repeat rolling and cutting with remaining balls of dough, stacking strips between plastic wrap and refrigerating as they are made.
3. Prepare Filling: Set aside 1/4 cup each nuts and raisins. In a food processor fitted with a chopping blade, process the remaining 3 cups walnuts until finely chopped. In a medium-sized bowl, combine the finely chopped nuts, sugar, and cinnamon. Set mixture aside.
4. Preheat oven to 325°F. Line one large baking sheet with aluminum foil; place two sheets of foil crosswise on a second large baking sheet. Place one 9-inch pastry ring (see Note) on the first baking sheet; place a 6-inch pastry ring and a 3-inch pastry ring on separate sheets of foil on the other baking sheet. With the tip of a skewer or toothpick, score an outline of all rings into foil; remove rings and set aside until ready to bake rugelach.
5. To make 9-inch rugelach, brush one strip of dough lightly with raspberry preserves. Sprinkle 3 tablespoons walnut mixture and 1 tablespoon raisins to cover half of the strip lengthwise. Fold the uncovered half of dough strip lengthwise over the covered half to enclose filling and make a 12 by 2-1/2-inch strip. Brush the top of the folded strip lightly with raspberry preserves. From either end, loosely roll up the filled strip; stand, cut edges up, in the center of the 9-inch ring scored on the baking sheet.
6. Fill and fold 8 more strips of dough as in step 5; brush the top of each with preserves. With the preserve-brushed surface facing the center, wrap one filled dough strip, cut edges up, around a rolled strip on the baking sheet. Continue wrapping remaining 7 filled strips around the center, pinching ends of strips together, until the ring is filled. Sprinkle the outside of rugelach with about 2 tablespoons walnut mixture and place the 9-inch pastry ring around the rugelach spiral.
7. With the remaining pastry strips, prepare 6-inch and 3-inch rugelachs repeating steps 5 and 6, using 5 filled strips of dough for 6-inch rugelach and 2 filled strips for 3-inch rugelach. Pat some of the remaining walnut mixture around the outside of the rugelachs, using about 1 1/2 tablespoons on each. Carefully place pastry rings around the rugelachs.
8. Bake 9-inch rugelach for 1 1/2 to 1 3/4 hours, 6-inch rugelach for 1 1/4 to 1 1/2 hours, and 3-inch rugelach for 1 hour or until the center is browned and firm to touch. With a large spatula, remove 3-inch rugelach, still on foil, to a wire rack allowing 6-inch rugelach to remain in the oven until completely baked. When baked, carefully transfer all rugelachs to a wire rack; remove pastry ring and foil. Let rugelachs cool to room temperature.
9. Just before serving, melt apricot preserves in a small saucepan and brush over the top of each rugelach. Place the 9-inch rugelach on a serving plate; top with the 6-inch and 3-inch rugelachs. If necessary, trim the tops of the 9 and 6-inch rugelachs to make them level so that tiers will stand securely. Garnish the plate with fresh ivy or other green leaves, if desired.
10. To serve, cut the 3-inch rugelach into 6 wedges and remove to individual plates. Then cut the 6-inch rugelach into 10 wedges and remove to plates; cut the 9-inch rugelach into 14 wedges and remove.
Note: Pastry rings, which look like bottomless tart pans, can be found at gourmet cookware stores. If not available in your area, order by mail from Bridge Kitchenware, 214 E. 52 Street, New York, N.Y. 10022 or call (212) 838-1901.
